channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden talks election, economy and Middle East in surprise news briefing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After 1,080 days as president, Joe Biden on Friday decided to pop in and take questions in the White House briefing room for the first time, striding in with a grin after a strong monthly jobs report and the temporary settlement of a strike by ports workers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he doesn't know whether Israel is holding up peace deal to influence 2024 US election
item.description: President Joe Biden has offered some terse words for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he doesn't know whether the Israeli leader is holding up a peace deal in order to influence the outcome of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Eric Adams's future in question as legal woes threaten to mount
item.description: Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams is fighting for his political life as he finds himself in the middle of a legal firestorm, raising questions about how much longer he can hold on to power. Adams was indicted last week by the Manhattan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Harris leads Trump by 2 in national poll, but shows vulnerabilities with non-White voters
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist Poll shows Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump are neck-and-neck nationwide with just over a month to go until the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to headline NRA event in pivotal swing state two weeks before Election Day
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the NRA's Defend the 2nd event in Savannah, Georgia, Oct. 22, rallying support for the Second Amendment two weeks before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disapproval mounts both at home and abroad as US avoids direct action against Houthi rebels
item.description: While much of the world has eyes on Israel’s battles with Hezbollah and Hamas, the U.S. Navy has its sights set on another of Iran’s proxies, the Yemeni Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris is making an unusual pilgrimage today — to the birthplace of the GOP
item.description: Harris will travel Thursday to Ripon, Wisconsin — home to the Little White Schoolhouse, where an 1854 meeting led to the birth of the Republican Party. She'll be joined by former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Marquette poll has Harris over Trump in Wisconsin, but within margin of error
item.description: Marquette University professor Charles Franklin joined the WGN Evening News on Wednesday to break down the latest numbers from the law school's poll about the upcoming presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ray Cortopassi, WGN 9

item.title: Melania Trump in new memoir calls abortion a 'woman's fundamental right': Report
item.description: “Why should anyone other than the woman herself have the power to determine what she does with her own body?" asks the former first lady.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats see signs of growing momentum in Texas Senate race
item.description: Democrats are seeing signs of growing momentum in the Texas Senate race between Sen. Ted Cruz (R) and Rep. Colin Allred (D), fueling the party’s hopes that this year could be the breakthrough they’ve been seeking for multiple cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis grants consent for Blue Line extension through city’s north side
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council signed off Wednesday on the proposed route of a light rail line that will run through a section of the city’s north side. However, some council members said they could only get to a yes vote after some of their concerns with the route were addressed.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, Matt Sepic, and Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Habitat for Humanity volunteers continue Carter’s legacy on 100th birthday
item.description: For 40 years, the project has brought together volunteers, celebrities and elected officials to build homes and highlight the need for affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Photos: As Jimmy Carter celebrates his 100th birthday, a look through the decades
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter is turning 100. Here are images from notable events or parts of his life, both during his presidency and after.
item.dc:creator: James Doubek, MPR News

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz CBS debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
